recommendations.reason: This course provides essential discrete mathematics concepts, foundational for advanced computer science topics and often a prerequisite for many upper-division courses.
Operating Systems is a core upper-division computer science course that will build upon your completed foundational programming and systems knowledge.
Digital Design is a crucial electrical and computer engineering course that will deepen your understanding of hardware systems, building on your completed ECEN 21.

summary: Based on your strong progress in foundational and early advanced CSE courses, these recommendations provide a balanced plan by addressing core mathematical reasoning, essential computer systems, and critical hardware design principles, setting you up for continued success in your major.
